---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Hayden Simm"
date: "2026-03-30"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

```{r}
killi_clean <- killi %>%
  drop_na(where(is.numeric))
```

PCA
```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(ggfortify)
})

# Step 1: Select relevant variables + grouping factors
killi_clean <- killi %>%
  select(
    COX_Activity,
    CS_Activity,
    LDH_Activity,
    Accliamtion_Temperature,
    Acclimation_Salinity,
    Assay_Temperature
  ) %>%
  # Remove rows with NA/Inf in PCA variables
  filter(if_all(
    c(COX_Activity, CS_Activity, LDH_Activity),
    ~ is.finite(.)
  )) %>%
  mutate(
    group = interaction(
      Accliamtion_Temperature,
      Acclimation_Salinity,
      Assay_Temperature
    ),
    # Step 1b: log-transform the activity variables
    COX_Activity = log(COX_Activity + 1e-6),  # add tiny constant to avoid log(0)
    CS_Activity  = log(CS_Activity  + 1e-6),
    LDH_Activity = log(LDH_Activity + 1e-6)
  )

# Step 2: Run PCA on log-transformed variables
pca <- prcomp(
  killi_clean %>% select(COX_Activity, CS_Activity, LDH_Activity),
  scale. = TRUE
)

# Step 3: Plot PCA
autoplot(
  pca,
  data = killi_clean,
  colour = "Accliamtion_Temperature",
  shape  = "Acclimation_Salinity",
  frame  = TRUE,
  frame.type = "norm"
) +
  facet_wrap(~ Assay_Temperature) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "PCA of Log-Transformed Metabolic Enzyme Activity",
    colour = "Acclimation Temp",
    shape  = "Acclimation Salinity"
  )

# Step 4: Outputs
summary(pca)
pca$rotation
```
*add a biplot for how each response loads into PC1 and PC2 space*

```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(vegan)
  library(ggrepel)
})

# Step 1: Prepare data + log transform
killi_rda <- killi %>%
  select(
    Fish_ID,
    CS_Activity,
    COX_Activity,
    LDH_Activity,
    Assay_Temperature,
    Accliamtion_Temperature,
    Acclimation_Salinity
  ) %>%
  filter(if_all(
    c(CS_Activity, COX_Activity, LDH_Activity),
    ~ is.finite(.)
  )) %>%
  mutate(
    Fish_ID = factor(Fish_ID),
    Assay_Temperature = factor(Assay_Temperature),
    Accliamtion_Temperature = factor(Accliamtion_Temperature),
    Acclimation_Salinity = factor(Acclimation_Salinity),

    # Log-transform (safe for zeros)
    across(
      c(CS_Activity, COX_Activity, LDH_Activity),
      ~ log(. + 1e-6)
    )
  )

# Step 2: Response matrix
Y <- killi_rda %>%
  select(CS_Activity, COX_Activity, LDH_Activity)

# Step 3: Run partial RDA (controls for Fish_ID)
rda_model <- rda(
  Y ~ Assay_Temperature *
    Accliamtion_Temperature *
    Acclimation_Salinity +
    Condition(Fish_ID),
  data = killi_rda
)

# Step 4: Significance tests
print(summary(rda_model))
print(anova(rda_model))
print(anova(rda_model, by = "terms"))
print(anova(rda_model, by = "axis"))

# Step 5: Extract scores
sites <- as.data.frame(scores(rda_model, display = "sites", scaling = 2))

species <- as.data.frame(scores(rda_model, display = "species", scaling = 2)) %>%
  mutate(label = rownames(.))   # critical fix for labels

sites <- bind_cols(sites, killi_rda)

# Step 6: Clean RDA plot (no faceting)
ggplot(sites, aes(
  x = RDA1, y = RDA2,
  colour = Acclimation_Temperature,
  shape  = Assay_Temperature
)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +

  # Enzyme vectors
  geom_segment(
    data = species,
    aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = RDA1, yend = RDA2),
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.2, "cm")),
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +

  # Repelled labels (clean + readable)
  geom_text_repel(
    data = species,
    aes(x = RDA1, y = RDA2, label = label),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 5,
    segment.color = "black",
    box.padding = 0.4,
    point.padding = 0.3,
    max.overlaps = Inf
  ) +

  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "RDA of Log-Transformed Enzyme Activities",
    x = "RDA1",
    y = "RDA2",
    colour = "Acclimation Temp",
    shape  = "Assay Temp"
  )

plot(rda_model, display = "sites", type = "n")  # empty plot
points(rda_model, display = "sites", col = "blue")  # residual site scores
```
